document_type.name: Generic Document
document_type.description: Any PDF document. Extract all visible sections, subsections, tables, lists, and content into structured JSON.

prompt: Extract document header/metadata information from this first page.
Look for ANY metadata typically found in document headers, title pages, or footers:
- Title or heading of the document
- Organisation, company, department, or agency name
1. Extract the EXACT text as it appears - do not paraphrase.
2. If a field is not found, use an empty string: ""
3. Look in: page headers, footers, title blocks, cover pages, front matter.
Return ONLY the JSON object (no markdown, start with {{):

definitions.section: A major section of the document with a distinct heading. Captures the heading text, all body paragraphs, and any nested subsections, tables, or lists within it.
definitions.table_section: A section that is primarily or entirely a table or grid of data, including the table title/caption if present.
definitions.list_section: A section that is primarily a numbered or bulleted list, including the list heading.
definitions.image_section: A section containing images, diagrams, figures, charts, or illustrations with captions.
definitions.front_matter: Title page, table of contents, executive summary, abstract, introduction, or other front matter.
definitions.back_matter: Appendices, references, bibliography, glossary, index, or other back matter.
definitions.unhandled_content: Content that does not clearly belong to any other section type, or boilerplate such as disclaimers, signatures, blank pages.

template: Analyze this PORTION of a document and identify its major SECTIONS.
1. Look for headings styled differently from body text (bold, larger font, numbered, underlined, coloured, or all-caps).
2. A heading followed by body text, a table, a list, or images = one section.
3. If the first page(s) are a cover/title page, table of contents, executive summary, or abstract, classify as 'front_matter'.
4. If the last pages are appendices, references, bibliography, glossary, or index, classify as 'back_matter'.
5. A standalone table with its own heading/caption = 'table_section'.
6. A standalone numbered/bulleted list with its own heading = 'list_section'.
7. Pages dominated by figures, charts, or diagrams = 'image_section'.
9. Boilerplate, disclaimers, signature blocks, blank pages = 'unhandled_content'.
- Extract the EXACT heading text from the document -- do NOT paraphrase
- If a section has no visible heading, use 'Untitled Section [page X]'
- Page numbers must be between {start_page} and {end_page}
- All pages must be covered -- no gaps
- Use ONLY ASCII characters (replace unicode dashes/bullets with ASCII equivalents)
- Return valid JSON only, no markdown or extra text

system_preamble: You are an expert data extraction agent specialising in EXACT text extraction from documents.
- Copy text EXACTLY word-for-word; preserve spelling, capitalisation, punctuation
- Use ONLY ASCII characters (0-127) -- replace unicode dashes/bullets with ASCII equivalents
- DO NOT paraphrase, summarise, or infer text that is not visible
- Empty fields: use '' for strings, [] for arrays, {{}} for objects
- Never use null, 'N/A', 'Unknown', or placeholder text
- Return ONLY valid JSON -- no markdown, no extra text
- Escape special characters properly in JSON strings
default_template: Extract ALL information from this document section into JSON.
Section: {section_name} ({section_type})
Pages: {start_page} to {end_page}
{image_info}
EXTRACTION RULES:
1. Extract ALL text from section '{section_name}' up to next section '{next_section_name}'.
2. If the section continues across pages, concatenate all content.
3. Empty arrays [] instead of [""].
4. Empty dicts {{}} instead of {{"key": ""}}.
HEADING AND BODY:
- 'heading' = the EXACT section heading text as a plain string.
- 'heading_level' = the apparent nesting depth: '1' for top-level, '2' for sub-section, '3' for sub-sub, etc.
- 'body' = array of strings; each new paragraph or line break = a new string in the array.
SUBSECTIONS:
- If you see sub-headings inside this section, nest them as objects in the 'subsections' array.
- Each subsection has its own heading, body, tables, lists, and images.
TABLES:
- Extract every table: caption (if any), header row as array of strings, and all data rows.
- Each row is an array of cell strings.
- Preserve the exact cell text; for merged cells, repeat content or leave empty as appropriate.
LISTS:
- Extract bulleted and numbered lists: list_title (if any) and items as an array of strings.
- Each bullet/number -> one string in the items array.
IMAGES:
- For images: match by PAGE and POSITION from the list above, copy the EXACT path into the 'image' field.
- Include captions if present in the 'text' field.
Look at ALL pages ({start_page} to {end_page}) and extract everything.
Return the complete JSON now (start with {{ or [, no markdown):
general_rules: - Each new paragraph or line break -> a new string in the body/items array.
- If a field has no content -> use '' for strings, [] for arrays, {{}} for objects

review_template: You are a document quality reviewer. You are provided with:
1. PDF PAGE IMAGES -- the ground truth for section '{section_name}'
2. EXTRACTED TEXT pulled from those pages
TASK -- compare the extracted text against the images and report:
1. WORD_ACCURACY: words in the extracted text that do not match the image.
Accuracy = (1 - mismatched/total_words)*100. >=95% high, >=85% medium, else low.
2. MISSING_INFORMATION_ACCURACY: words visible in the image but absent from extracted text.
Accuracy = (1 - missing/total_words)*100. >=95% high, >=85% medium, else low.
3. DUPLICATION_ACCURACY: entire sections that are repeated (not individual words).
Count >=1 duplicated sections -> low; else high.
image_description_prompt: Analyze this image from the '{section_name}' section.
Provide a SPECIFIC description (max {description_len} words) with any visible text or colours.

image_context_hints._default: Describe this image briefly, noting any text, labels, or key visual elements.
